title: After-Action Report — WAR / OAA reconciliation (Memphis Koalas)
scope: Scope: read-only audit. No engine, rating, or stat changes in this commit — this is the *finding* record written **before** any mitigation, per the "document the ask and the honest gaps first" convention.

rationale.item: A Memphis Koalas center fielder shows WAR 3.65 on the season card but OAA −25.5 / Field Runs −19.9 on the O27 Index page. The owner asked: does the WAR actually consume the OAA/Field-Runs penalty, or is it reading a different (lighter) fielding input? If the two surfaces pull defense from different sources, that's the bug.

Root cause (owner's context): these stats were built early in development; new metrics were layered in, but the WAR aggregation was never re-pointed at them. The breakage is drift, not a single bad edit.

delegation_events.decision: Audit only; mitigations in a subsequent commit
delegation_events.quote: No engine, rating, formula, or stat changes — audit only, as directed.

actions.description: Read-only audit tracing the WAR formula (two terms only: VORP_bat + scout DRS), the Koalas arithmetic, OAA attribution artifacts in build_fielding_value(), the xwOBA luck-gap check, and invariant coverage; this finding record written

risk_posture: read-only audit. No engine, rating, or stat changes in this commit
The honest statement: 3.65 WAR is correct given the inputs it actually uses — it simply does not incorporate the simulated fielding at all, so it cannot be "wrong" about a penalty it never applied.

validation_claims.claim: The season-card WAR's defensive term is a scout-rating-derived DRS; it never reads the batted-ball event log that OAA/Field Runs are built from
validation_claims.check_method: Read the WAR block at o27v2/web/app.py:1626-1648 and _position_defense_for_row

validation_claims.claim: 3.65 ≈ +3.5 bat + ~+0.1 scout-defense; feeding −19.9 Field Runs through /rpw would give dWAR ≈ −1.3 to −2.0 and WAR near 1.6–2.3
validation_claims.check_method: Run the comprehensive report's query on the live DB (exact VORP/rpw split needs the live DB)

validation_claims.claim: Post-inversion (2026-06-01) 100% of batted balls are physics-decided; this player's +0.108 wOBA−xwOBA gap across 155/155 physics-decided batted balls is genuine over-performance
validation_claims.check_method: Check build_xwoba_ev_table and the physics-first inversion AAR; query the player's batted balls

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_stat_invariants.py has 10 invariants; none checks WAR against its own components
Only ~41% of chances are exactly attributed

non_actions.item: No engine, rating, formula, or stat changes
Did not pin the exact VORP/rpw numeric split
non_actions.reason: no live DB in the sandbox; the comprehensive report supplies the exact query to run on the owner's DB
Mitigations follow in a subsequent commit, after this record lands.

residual_risks: Neither WAR's scout-DRS term nor OAA is a trustworthy fielding truth: the rating prior structurally cannot see range; event-based OAA over-charges everyday regulars.

OAA miscalibration vectors: HRs and extra-base hits counted as fielding chances; out-vs-hit attribution asymmetry (spray-blind _select_fielder for outs vs actual trajectory zone for hits); only ~41% of chances exactly attributed.
A "surfaced component must equal the value that flows into WAR" invariant would fail immediately today (WAR omits baserunning, omits a standalone positional term, reads fielding from scout DRS).
Luck-gap caveat: games predating the 2026-06-01 inversion carry echo-EV and should be excluded before trusting the gap on those rows.
open_hooks: The comprehensive companion report carries the mitigation roadmap and the proposed invariant.
Exact VORP/rpw query to run on the owner's DB.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — WAR / OAA reconciliation (Memphis Koalas)
TL;DR — they pull defense from different sources. That's the bug.
Is OAA −25.5 even real? Suspect — an attribution artifact, not a range grade.
The luck gap is real (lower-priority question)
What I did NOT do (this commit)
unmapped_content: WAR formula: WAR = VORP_bat/rpw + DRS_scout/rpw; VORP_bat = (wOBA − 0.85·league_wOBA) × PA / 1.20; DRS_scout = (pos_def − 0.5) × 2 × (G/162) × range[pos] (CF range 8.0); pos_def = 0.6·sub_grade + 0.4·general_grade. Of the owner's five components only two are in this WAR (no OAA/Field Runs, no BSR term, no standalone positional adjustment — folded into _POSITION_DRS_RANGE). Koalas arithmetic: DRS = (pos_def − 0.5) × 6.12 runs; dWAR ≈ +0.04…+0.12 (rpw ≈ 13–18). −25.5 over 62 G ≈ −0.41 OAA/game vs worst qualified MLB defender ~−0.13/game. FldPct .989 and OAA −25.5 can both be "true" because they measure different things. Branch claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG.
parser_notes: Finding-only AAR (audit, no code changes); companion to docs/aar-stat-surface-reconciliation-comprehensive.md and predecessor of docs/aar-war-oaa-fix-and-fielding-regression.md. Date from 'Date:' header.
